Title: Soluble and insoluble α-glucan synthesis in yeast by enzyme suites derived exclusively from maize endosperm

Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: yeast GAL1 or CWP2 promoters (galactose-active; GAL1 inducible, CWP2 constitutive/active in galactose)
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: developing endosperm mRNA expression used for gene selection
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: induced during growth in galactose medium (YPGal)
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: yeast whole-genome resequencing/Illumina NovaSeq 6000 to verify exogenous gene integrity; accession NA
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: insoluble α-glucan (IPG; starch-like glucan particle) and soluble α-glucan/SPG partitioning
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: plant starch-like carbon storage polymer; semi-crystalline particle formation
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: yeast cytosol as insoluble particles filling much of the cell
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: galactose induction in YPGal medium; 30°C; 5.75 h culture
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: heterologous expression in yeast; glucan fractionation and quantification of IPG/SPG/MOS; iodine staining; CLD analysis; SEM/TEM; comparison of strains with and without ISA1
Common_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: cytosol in engineered yeast (plastid transit peptide removed from synthetic construct)
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: strongly promotes redistribution of α-glucan from soluble SPG to insoluble IPG; often increases proportion of insoluble glucan to ~72–84% in multi-SS backgrounds; can reduce total α-glucan in some single-SS backgrounds
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Maize ISA1 functions in yeast without ISA2 and stimulates insoluble α-glucan formation. 2. ISA1 shifts glucan partitioning from SPG to IPG in multiple maize enzyme backgrounds. 3. ISA1 is associated with MOS accumulation and increased abundance of starch-like particles, but does not substantially alter IPG/SPG CLD profiles at the resolution measured.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Unlike Arabidopsis ISA, maize ISA1 did not require ISA2 for activity in this system. ISA1 was not essential for particle formation but dramatically increased particle abundance and insoluble fraction. In some backgrounds ISA1 lowered total glucan, suggesting context-dependent degradation/remodeling of SPG.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: In several backgrounds including single SS isoforms, ISA1 reduced total α-glucan accumulation, especially by lowering SPG
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for engineering starch-like insoluble α-glucan particle formation and increasing crystallization/insolubility; likely valuable in gene stacks with SS and SBE suites rather than alone

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: developing endosperm mRNA expression used for selection
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: yeast whole-genome resequencing/Illumina NovaSeq 6000 to verify transgenes; accession NA
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: combinatorial heterologous expression in yeast; iodine staining; glucan quantification comparing SSV-containing and SSV-lacking strains
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: starch synthase V/SS5-like noncanonical starch synthase paralog lacking conserved catalytic glucosyltransferase motifs
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: does not itself produce detectable α-glucan, but enhances total α-glucan accumulation synergistically in the SSI+PHO1 background
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SSV alone did not support detectable α-glucan accumulation. 2. Adding SSV to SSI+PHO1 significantly increased total α-glucan (~1.6-fold). 3. Results support a noncatalytic accessory role, potentially in granule initiation or stimulation of SSI-dependent synthesis.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper interprets maize SSV as likely noncatalytic, analogous to Arabidopsis SS5. Synergistic effect was background-dependent and not observed across all enzyme combinations.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Limited standalone utility because no detectable polymer synthesis by itself
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential accessory gene for starch engineering when stacked with catalytic SS isoforms and PHO1; may aid initiation-related functions

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: developing endosperm mRNA expression used for gene selection
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: yeast whole-genome resequencing/Illumina NovaSeq 6000 to verify transgene integrity; accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: branch formation in amylopectin-like α-glucan assembly
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: introduces α(1→6) branch points into α(1→4)-linked glucan chains during starch-like polymer assembly
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for storage/modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: heterologous expression in yeast; iodine staining with AtSS3 background; glucan quantification
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: functional in yeast; with AtSS3 increases total α-glucan ~4–5-fold over AtSS3 alone and supports high IPG proportion; with ISA1 did not further shift partitioning as strongly as SBEII isoforms
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SBEI alone did not support α-glucan accumulation. 2. In combination with SS activity, SBEI increased polymer accumulation, confirming enzyme functionality in yeast. 3. Together with AtSS3, SBEI supported predominantly insoluble glucan formation even without ISA1.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: One of three maize SBEs proven functional in yeast. Individual SBE backgrounds differed in ISA1 responsiveness.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful core enzyme for reconstructing branched starch-like polymers in heterologous systems

Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: yeast whole-genome resequencing/Illumina NovaSeq 6000; accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: transfers glucan segments to create α(1→6) branches in α-glucan polymers
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for storage/modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: heterologous expression in yeast; iodine staining with AtSS3; glucan quantification
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: functional in yeast and elevates α-glucan accumulation with SS activity; ISA1 further shifts glucan toward insoluble IPG in SBEIIa background
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SBEIIa alone was insufficient for α-glucan accumulation. 2. In presence of AtSS3, SBEIIa markedly increased total α-glucan. 3. With ISA1, glucan partitioning shifted further toward insoluble IPG.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Previously shown functional in yeast in earlier work; current study confirmed within maize-only SBE suite context.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Relevant branch-forming enzyme for starch structure engineering

Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: yeast whole-genome resequencing/Illumina NovaSeq 6000; accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: introduces α(1→6) branches into elongating α-glucan chains
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for storage/modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: heterologous expression in yeast; iodine staining with AtSS3; glucan quantification
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: functional in yeast and increases α-glucan content with SS activity; ISA1 further promotes insoluble IPG in this background
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SBEIIb alone did not produce detectable α-glucan. 2. In combination with AtSS3, SBEIIb caused strong increases in polymer accumulation. 3. ISA1 further shifted glucan distribution toward insoluble IPG.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: All three maize SBE isoforms were functional; combining all three did not exceed single-SBE effects in total glucan under tested conditions.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful in engineered branching modules for starch-like polymer synthesis

Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: yeast whole-genome resequencing/Illumina NovaSeq 6000; accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: elongates α(1→4)-glucan chains using ADP-glucose but requires/benefits from suitable primer structures
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: combinatorial heterologous expression; iodine staining; glucan quantification across SSI-alone and SSI+PHO1/SSIV/SSV backgrounds
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: alone did not support detectable polymer accumulation; shows strong synergistic stimulation with PHO1 and with SSIV, and weaker context-specific synergy with SSV
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SSI alone was insufficient to accumulate detectable α-glucan. 2. SSI plus PHO1 strongly increased total glucan, indicating functional SSI and synergy. 3. SSI also displayed strong synergism with SSIV and context-dependent synergy with SSV.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Results support primer dependence of SSI activity in vivo; proposed that PHO1 or SSIV may provide glucan primers.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Low standalone activity in this system
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Best deployed in stacks with PHO1 and/or SSIV rather than as a solitary enzyme

Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: yeast whole-genome resequencing/Illumina NovaSeq 6000; accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: extends glucan chains during starch-like polymer synthesis; in this system sufficient to initiate/support polymer accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: major productive synthase in this system; highest total α-glucan among individual SSs tested
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: heterologous expression; iodine staining; glucan quantification; CLD analysis of IPG from SSIIa-only strains
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: alone supports substantial α-glucan accumulation and high total polymer levels; PHO1 decreases total glucan in SSIIa background; ISA1 shifts glucan toward insoluble IPG without increasing total glucan
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SSIIa alone supported strong α-glucan synthesis and was the most productive single SS isoform tested. 2. PHO1 reduced SSIIa-derived glucan, suggesting degradative action in this context. 3. ISA1 increased insoluble IPG proportion, and SSIIa alone synthesized chains reaching at least DP 40.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Study argues SSIIa has adequate initiation capability in this heterologous system. Produced CLD still differed from native maize amylopectin.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: PHO1 coexpression can lower total polymer accumulation in SSIIa backgrounds
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate catalytic core for engineering starch-like polymer production, especially when paired with SBEs and optionally ISA1 for insolubility

Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: yeast whole-genome resequencing/Illumina NovaSeq 6000; accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: elongates glucan chains and supports polymer synthesis in the absence of other SS isoforms
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: not established, but highly productive individual synthase
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: heterologous expression; iodine staining; glucan quantification; CLD analysis of SSIIIa-only IPG
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: alone supports substantial glucan accumulation, mostly soluble; PHO1 lowers total glucan and ISA1 can lower total glucan while altering partitioning
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SSIIIa alone supported substantial α-glucan synthesis. 2. PHO1 reduced total glucan in the SSIIIa background. 3. SSIIIa acting alone synthesized chains extending to at least DP 40.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Produced predominantly soluble polymer relative to SSIIa; CLD competence overlaps SSIIa, arguing against strict isoform-specific chain-length ceilings.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: PHO1 and ISA1 can reduce total glucan in this background
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful productive SS isoform for reconstitution studies, though polymer partitioning may require additional enzymes to favor insoluble product

Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: yeast whole-genome resequencing/Illumina NovaSeq 6000; accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: elongates α-glucan chains and in this system supports initiation/accumulation of polymer when combined with SBEs
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: heterologous expression; iodine staining; glucan quantification; analysis of synergy with SSI and effect of PHO1/ISA1
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: with SBEs supports detectable α-glucan accumulation; displays strong synergistic interaction with SSI; PHO1 and ISA1 each reduce total glucan in SSIV backgrounds
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SSIV was functional in yeast and supported polymer synthesis with SBE background. 2. SSIV acted synergistically with SSI, yielding 2- to ~6-fold more glucan than expected from single-enzyme sums. 3. PHO1 reduced SSIV-derived glucan, and ISA1 often lowered total glucan while affecting partitioning.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Discussion links SSIV to granule initiation functions, but study also shows SSIIa can substitute for initiation in this system.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Coexpression with PHO1 or ISA1 may reduce total glucan in some combinations
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potentially valuable in initiation-oriented engineering stacks, especially with SSI

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: developing endosperm; activity developmentally coincident with rapid starch accumulation according to discussion
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: yeast whole-genome resequencing/Illumina NovaSeq 6000; accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: α(1→4)-glucan and phosphate, or glucose-1-phosphate depending on reaction direction
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: glucose-1-phosphate or elongated α-glucan depending on direction
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: reversibly adds glucosyl units from glucose-1-phosphate to α-glucan chains or phosphorolytically degrades α-glucan to glucose-1-phosphate
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: can supply precursor/primer pool or compete with target-product accumulation depending on enzyme context
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: context-dependent control point, not universally rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: heterologous expression; iodine staining; glucan quantification across multiple SS combinations
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: has isoform-dependent dual effects: strongly stimulates α-glucan accumulation with SSI, decreases total glucan with SSIIa/SSIIIa/SSIV, and has little net effect in some multi-SS combinations
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PHO1 alone did not support detectable α-glucan accumulation. 2. PHO1 synergized strongly with SSI, consistent with a synthetic/primer-providing role. 3. PHO1 reduced glucan accumulation with SSIIa, SSIIIa, and SSIV, supporting a degradative role in those contexts. 4. PHO1 effects disappeared or were balanced in some multi-SS backgrounds.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Study provides direct evidence for both synthetic and degradative PHO1 roles depending on accompanying SS isoforms.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Can substantially reduce polymer accumulation in some enzyme combinations
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Should be deployed only in context-aware stacks; beneficial with SSI-like modules but potentially detrimental with SSIIa/SSIIIa/SSIV-dominant systems
